skill.name: core-development
skill.description: Guides development of the core DSL package, including types, validation, normalization, diff logic, patches, and focused tests.
skill.summary: Develop core DSL processing with guidance for validation, normalization, diff behavior, patches, and tests.
skill.author: 7nohe

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged many Ruby or shell backtick patterns, weak cryptography patterns, and one reconnaissance pattern. Review found these are Markdown code spans, diagrams, TypeScript examples, and benign local test commands, with no evidence of malicious intent or prompt injection.

risk_factor_evidence.assessment: The skill documents bun test commands for local package testing. These are legitimate development commands and are not automatically executed by the skill file.

low_findings.title: Markdown Backtick Static Matches Are False Positives
low_findings.description: The Ruby or shell backtick findings point to Markdown inline code, fenced diagrams, TypeScript examples, and file names. They are documentation text, not executable Ruby or shell code.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The referenced locations are Markdown code spans or fenced blocks. No executable script, shell interpolation, or runtime command wrapper is present in the skill file.

low_findings.title: Benign Local Test Command Guidance
low_findings.description: The only actual shell commands are bun test examples for running core package tests. They are scoped to local test execution and do not access the network or secrets.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The command examples are real external commands, but their purpose is legitimate testing. There is no evidence of command injection, obfuscation, persistence, or exfiltration.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Matches Are False Positives
low_findings.description: The weak cryptographic algorithm findings do not correspond to cryptographic code or algorithm use. The referenced lines contain the skill description and a type diagram entry.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The reviewed lines contain no hash, cipher, random number generation, or cryptographic API. The static signal appears to be a substring match in documentation.

low_findings.title: System Reconnaissance Match Is False Positive
low_findings.description: The system reconnaissance finding points to a workflow item about updating validation logic. It does not instruct the agent to enumerate the host, environment, users, or network.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The line is a development checklist item for validate.ts. No system discovery command or reconnaissance intent is present.

content.value_statement: Core DSL changes can break validation, normalization, or patch output. This skill guides Claude, Codex, and Claude Code through core package data flow and tests.
actual_capabilities: Explains the core package data flow from YAML DSL input to patch output.
Identifies the main TypeScript files for types, validation, normalization, and diff logic.
Guides changes to DSL and IR type definitions with matching validation updates.
Describes patch operation structures used for node and edge changes.
Provides focused Bun test commands for the core package and specific test files.
limitations: Covers only the core package and does not document UI or plugin implementation details.
Does not provide full source code for validators, normalizers, or diff algorithms.
Assumes the repository already uses Bun and co-located TypeScript tests.
Does not include automated migration guidance for existing DSL documents.

use_cases.title: Add DSL Fields
use_cases.description: Use the skill to update DSL and IR types, add validation, define defaults, and adjust diff behavior.

use_cases.title: Debug Patch Output
use_cases.description: Use the data flow and patch operation notes to trace why node or edge changes produce unexpected patches.

use_cases.title: Review Core Changes
use_cases.description: Use the checklist to verify that type, validation, normalization, diff, and test changes stay aligned.

prompt_templates.title: Find the Right Core File
prompt_templates.prompt: I need to change core DSL behavior. Use this skill to identify which core files likely need updates and why.

prompt_templates.title: Plan a New Node Field
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this skill to plan a new DSL node field. Include type, validation, normalization, diff, and test updates.

prompt_templates.title: Diagnose Diff Behavior
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this skill to inspect a diff behavior change. Explain the data flow and list focused tests to run.

prompt_templates.title: Review a Core Refactor
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this skill to review a core package refactor for validation gaps, normalization regressions, patch compatibility, and missing tests.

output_examples.input: Add a new optional label field to DSL edges.
output: Update the edge type definition first.
Add validation rules for accepted label values.
Define the normalized default when the label is absent.
Update edge equality behavior if labels affect patches.
Add validation, normalization, and diff test coverage.

output_examples.input: A node move does not appear in generated patches.
output: Trace the YAML DSL through validation and normalization.
Compare the previous and next IR node layout values.
Inspect diff logic for layout equality checks.
Run the focused diff test file after adding a regression case.

output_examples.input: Review a pull request that changes normalize behavior.
output: Check that defaults are documented by tests.
Confirm validation rejects unsupported input before normalization.
Verify patch output remains stable for unchanged documents.

best_practices: Update types, validation, normalization, diff logic, and tests together for schema-related changes.
Add focused tests for every new default, validation rule, or equality condition.
Run the core package test suite before merging changes that alter DSL processing.
anti_patterns: Changing normalized IR shape without updating validation and diff expectations.
Adding defaults that are not covered by normalization tests.
Treating patch output changes as incidental when they affect clients.

faq.question: What package does this skill cover?
faq.answer: It covers the core package that handles DSL types, validation, normalization, diff logic, and patch operations.

faq.question: Does this skill execute code automatically?
faq.answer: No. It documents local test commands, but any command execution depends on the user or agent workflow.

faq.question: Which tools can use this skill?
faq.answer: The report lists support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.

faq.question: When should I use this skill?
faq.answer: Use it when modifying DSL processing, IR conversion, patch generation, or related core package tests.

faq.question: Does it explain WebSocket implementation?
faq.answer: It lists protocol message types, but it does not cover runtime WebSocket implementation details.

faq.question: What tests does it recommend?
faq.answer: It recommends the core test suite and focused tests for diff, validation, and normalization files.
